summary:
Intraoral dental X-rays do not have embedded protective features such as lead foils; they rely purely on controlled radiographic techniques during use. The other options accurately describe typical components or functions without involving unnecessary safety measures inherent only to more complex medical imaging systems. Option [B] incorrectly suggests this feature exists. This summary captures both core facts succinctly while focusing directly on what distinguishes correct from erroneous claims related specifically to standard practice among intraoral xray devices.
